---
title: "Complementary pathways"
subtitle: "How the Freshwater Science degree pairs with Chemistry, Biology, and other directions"
date: last-modified
---

Many students who pick Freshwater Science want a *focus* on top of the
broad core — an applied direction they can take into work or graduate
school. The patterns below come from the program lead's curated
advising map (`docs/reference/advising/Map UBCO Freshwater Science
(BSc) - Pathways.docx`). Use them as starting points; the program
advisor will sharpen the specific course choices to your goals.

# Water quality and biogeochemistry

A Chemistry-leaning track that builds applied analytical and
contaminant-cycling expertise.

- **Core EESC**: EESC 301 (Limnology), EESC 309 (Global
  Biogeochemistry), EESC 423 (Tracers of Natural Processes).
- **Core CHEM**: CHEM 211 (Analytical), CHEM 301 (Aqueous
  Environmental Chemistry).
- **GISC Minor** to add spatial analysis and remote sensing.
- **Suits**: contaminant monitoring, redox / speciation work, trace
  analysis in waters, water-utility lab roles.
- **Lab home**: UBCO Chemistry FiLTER (Field Lab for Trace Element
  Research) is a frequent collaborator.

# Aquatic ecology and conservation

A Biology-leaning track for biomonitoring, fisheries, and
species-level work.

- **Core EESC**: EESC 301 (Limnology), EESC 402 (Freshwater
  Resources).
- **Core BIOL**: BIOL 308 (Population Biology), BIOL 375 / FWSC 375
  (Flora & Fauna of Inland Waters).
- **Year-4 capstone**: Directed Studies or EESC 449 (Honours Thesis).
- **Co-op targets**: provincial and federal biology agencies,
  fisheries consulting, NGOs.
- **Designation fit**: this is the natural pairing with the
  [RPBio pathway](rpbio.qmd).

# Hydrology and water resources

A Civil-Engineering-adjacent track for watershed modelling,
flood/drought risk, and water infrastructure.

- **Core EESC**: EESC 413 (Analytical Methods in Hydrology),
  EESC 435 (Fluvial Field Techniques), EESC 423 (Tracers),
  EESC 342 (Hydrogeology).
- **ENGR pairing**: ENGR 342 (Open Channel Flow) — *seat and
  prerequisite limits apply; coordinate with Engineering advising*.
- **GISC Minor** for geospatial modelling.
- **Co-op targets**: hydrometric agencies (ECCC, WSC), engineering
  consulting (NHC, Associated Engineering, etc.), utilities.

# Practical notes for mixing and matching

- **Co-op across programs.** Co-op is available in Freshwater,
  Chemistry, Biology, and Engineering — ideal for stitching together
  applied experience.
- **Credit double-counting.** The UBCO Academic Calendar caps
  double-counting of upper-level credits toward a second major or
  minor (generally max 6 credits for program-specified requirements).
  Schedule intentionally if you're aiming for a double credential.
- **Advising.** Use the official Program Plan and meet program
  advisors early — especially if you're eyeing Honours, co-op, or
  cross-faculty electives.

# References

- [UBCO Freshwater Science (program page)](https://you.ubc.ca/ubc_programs/freshwater-science/)
- [UBC Academic Calendar — Freshwater Science requirements](https://okanagan.calendar.ubc.ca/faculties-schools-and-colleges/faculty-science/bachelor-science-programs/major-programs/freshwater-science)
- [UBCO BSc Freshwater Science — Program Plan (PDF)](https://students.ok.ubc.ca/wp-content/uploads/sites/90/2025/06/BSc-Freshwater-Science_Program-Plan.pdf)
- [UBCO Chemistry — undergraduate page](https://chemistry.ok.ubc.ca/undergraduate/)
- [UBCO Civil Engineering — programs](https://engineering.ok.ubc.ca/programs-admissions/civil/)
- [UBCO GISC Minor](https://eesc.ok.ubc.ca/undergraduate/gisc-minor/)
- Source notes in this repo: `docs/reference/advising/Map UBCO Freshwater Science (BSc) - Pathways.docx`.
